title: Remove wrong-project navigation graph task
description: Delete the erroneous navigation-graph Beads issue because it belongs to the wrong project category and should not remain as open or closed work in this repository.
design: Use Beads issue deletion for the erroneous navigation-graph task only, then refresh the tracked .beads/issues.jsonl snapshot and close this tracking task.
acceptance_criteria: - The erroneous navigation-graph task no longer appears in Beads issue listings\n- Remaining Beads status has no open or in-progress issues from this cleanup\n- .beads/issues.jsonl is refreshed through Beads, not edited by hand
notes: User explicitly said the navigation graph task sounds like a wrong project category mistake and asked to delete that one.

title: Investigate and close resolved open Beads items
description: User asked to investigate the remaining unclosed Beads items and close any that are already resolved. Scope is issue-state review plus any narrow verification needed to determine whether each item is closeable; avoid broader implementation unless a close decision requires a small confirming probe.
design: Inspect Beads open/non-closed items with bd, read issue details and referenced files as needed, run the fastest relevant checks for close candidates, then close only items whose acceptance criteria are already satisfied. Leave unresolved items open with notes or follow-up context.
acceptance_criteria:
- Identify all currently unclosed Beads items
- Close items whose acceptance criteria are already satisfied
- Leave unresolved items open with clear handoff context
- Report verification performed and a conventional commit message if tracked files changed
notes: User request on 2026-05-02: investigate the 3 Beads items that have not been closed yet and close if possible.

title: Tighten smoke test missing-file assertions
description: scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh can print rg IO warnings and still pass when forbid_text is called on a missing temp scaffold file. This can hide missing generated files or make smoke output noisy.
design: Adjust smoke-test helpers or temp scaffold assertions so required generated files are checked before forbid_text, and missing files fail loudly instead of being treated like absent forbidden text. Focus scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh only.
acceptance_criteria: - Smoke test emits no rg IO warnings for missing temp scaffold files\n- Missing files checked by forbid_text fail loudly or are required first\n- scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh still passes
notes: Discovered during agb-ecx while running scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh; output included rg IO errors for temp .claude/anti-patterns.md but the smoke test exited 0.

title: Fix bootstrap scaffold cadence wording
description: Update repository instructions so /bootstrap is described as initial/project hydration, not as a required follow-up after every scripts/scaffold.sh refresh.
design: Find the source for the Scaffold Hydration section, likely AGENTS.md or scaffold templates. Update wording to distinguish first-time or stale metadata hydration from routine forward scaffold refreshes. Add/update the required lightweight plan artifact if the repo has the expected .claude planning structure.
acceptance_criteria:
- Instructions no longer imply /bootstrap must run after every scaffold refresh
- scripts/scaffold.sh remains documented as the forward refresh path
- /bootstrap remains documented for initial hydration or intentional re-hydration when project facts/templates need regeneration
- Run configured feedback loops or explicitly skip if not configured
notes: User corrected previous interpretation: requiring /bootstrap after every scaffold run conflicts with the skill's intended bootstrapping semantics.

title: Clarify bootstrap skill semantics
description: Clarify intended semantics of /bootstrap versus scripts/scaffold.sh after user noted that requiring /bootstrap after every scaffold refresh conflicts with the bootstrap concept.
design: Discuss the distinction between one-time repo hydration and routine forward scaffold refreshes; propose wording if useful, without editing files unless requested.
acceptance_criteria: - Acknowledge the prior interpretation if it was too broad\n- State a clearer intended contract for /bootstrap\n- Identify whether AGENTS.md wording should be changed
notes: User said requiring /bootstrap after every scaffold script run goes against their intent for the /bootstrap skill name and concept.

title: Clarify scaffold bootstrap cadence
description: Clarify whether /bootstrap is required after every scripts/scaffold.sh run, based on the repository AGENTS.md scaffold hydration contract. No code or docs changes are planned.
design: Answer from AGENTS.md instructions only; no file edits unless the user asks for a documentation change.
acceptance_criteria: - Explain when /bootstrap is required after scripts/scaffold.sh\n- Distinguish initial scaffold hydration from routine forward scaffold refreshes\n- Avoid changing tracked files
notes: User asked: am I understanding this right but is /bootstrap required after every run of the scaffold script?

title: Render Beads bootstrap commands in AGENTS scaffold
description: The universal AGENTS.md scaffold template hardcodes bd bootstrap --yes --json even though scripts/scaffold.sh already infers bootstrap_commands from .beads/clone-contract.json. Refreshing gloggur-headquarters rewrote its repo-specific bd init/import/githooks clone contract and broke its pre-push contract tests.
design: Expose BEADS_BOOTSTRAP_COMMANDS_JSON as a rendered multiline template variable, update AGENTS.md.tmpl to render that variable, and include the session-close bd dolt push guard and .githooks support line so downstream repo-owned Beads contracts are preserved on scaffold refresh.
acceptance_criteria: - AGENTS.md.tmpl no longer hardcodes bd bootstrap --yes --json for fresh clone docs\n- Scaffolded AGENTS.md uses bootstrap_commands from an existing .beads/clone-contract.json\n- Scaffolded AGENTS.md includes the bd dolt push session-close guard and .githooks support statement
notes: Discovered while fixing gloggur-headquarters ghq-z9d1 after a scaffold refresh broke tests/test_git_beads_scripts.py.

title: Draft commit message for runtime env guidance
description: Provide a conventional commit message for the current AGENTS template change that adds runtime environment variable delivery-path verification guidance.
design: Inspect the current git diff and summarize the change without modifying source templates.
acceptance_criteria: - Commit message uses conventional commit format\n- Body explains why the guidance was added\n- No source implementation changes are made
notes: Current evidence: git diff shows one AGENTS.md.tmpl bullet added about verifying runtime env delivery path and adding a contract test.

title: Draft staged commit message
description: Inspect the currently staged git diff and provide a conventional commit message. Scope is read-only git inspection plus response text; no source edits.
design: Use git status and git diff --cached to determine staged intent, then return a concise conventional commit message.
acceptance_criteria: - Staged changes are inspected\n- Commit message reflects staged intent\n- No source files are edited
notes: User requested a conventional commit message for staged changes.

title: Reinforce adopted-artifact questionnaire depth
description: A full /resolve-adopted-artifacts session showed the skill already requires an adoption interview, but the questionnaire can still be too thin when several independent policy decisions are grouped together. Tighten the upstream skill template so future runs ask a more thorough set of decision questions before cleanup, especially when a user-facing prompt groups multiple rule families.
design: Make a small wording-only update in bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl near Step 4. Reinforce that grouped prompts must still enumerate every unresolved rule family as separate numbered sub-questions, and that the checkpoint should err toward a second short question round rather than compressing uncertain decisions. Add a smoke-test assertion only if needed to lock the new instruction.
acceptance_criteria: - resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl explicitly requires thorough questionnaire coverage for each independent unresolved rule family\n- The change stays small and does not add new adoption workflow machinery\n- Existing scaffold smoke test still passes or is updated with a focused assertion
notes: User retro feedback: the previous adoption interview was useful, but /resolve-adopted-artifacts could have given an even more thorough questionnaire. They requested only a small upstream template modification.
close_reason: Added a focused resolve-adopted-artifacts instruction requiring more complete questionnaires for grouped policy decisions, with smoke coverage.

title: Draft commit message for staged changes
description: Inspect the currently staged git diff and provide a concise conventional commit message for the user. Scope is read-only git inspection plus handoff text; no source edits.
design: Use git diff --cached and related read-only status commands to identify the staged change intent, then produce one high-signal conventional commit message.
acceptance_criteria: - Staged changes are inspected\n- Conventional commit message matches the staged change intent\n- No tracked source files are edited
notes: Requested directly by user from IDE context; active files include bootstrap template and beads snapshot.

title: Tighten resolve-adopted-artifacts policy extraction
description: The resolve-adopted-artifacts skill currently allows the agent to compress high-signal AGENTS.md conflicts into a hand-wavy summary such as 'stricter repo rules'. That defeats the skill's core purpose: extracting each legacy rule, identifying the reusable scaffold lesson or local parameter, and grilling the user on which details to incorporate upstream.
design: Update bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl so policy conflicts, especially AGENTS.md, must be presented as grouped line-item findings with concrete source evidence, loss/change impact, candidate upstream disposition, and focused follow-up questions. Add regression checks in scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh that assert the template forbids catch-all summaries and requires continuation questions for bundled rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- The skill template explicitly rejects umbrella summaries like 'stricter repo rules' for policy artifacts
- The skill requires decomposing bundled policy loss into individual rules before the user-facing interview
- The interview format requires grouped, concrete follow-up questions about what to incorporate upstream
- Smoke tests assert the new template guardrails
notes: Discovered while running /resolve-adopted-artifacts in gloggur-headquarters: the response summarized AGENTS.md loss as a compact list rather than cutting into each rule and grilling the user on upstream incorporation details.
close_reason: Updated resolve-adopted-artifacts template to force decomposed policy extraction/interview and added smoke assertions.

title: Preserve adopter scaffold variables and hook behavior
description: When agent-bootstrap is first applied to an existing Beads-enabled repository, scripts/scaffold.sh can generate scaffold candidates that would silently change existing behavior: replacing a real issue prefix with the default prj, replacing an explicit clone recovery contract with the generic bd bootstrap command, and producing a pre-commit candidate that drops repo-owned validation/lint commands. These should be handled in the upstream scaffold implementation wherever the scaffold can infer and preserve existing behavior, rather than forcing target projects to patch candidates by hand.
design: Inspect scripts/scaffold.sh variable derivation and target generation. Add evidence-backed inference from existing repository artifacts before defaults are used, especially BEADS_PREFIX from .beads/config.yaml or .beads/clone-contract.json and bootstrap commands from existing clone-contract state. Add a safe hook extension mechanism or preservation path for existing .githooks/pre-commit commands so generated candidates do not drop local hook behavior without an explicit extension point. Update templates and smoke tests as needed to prove scaffold-generated candidates preserve inferred behavior and expose local hook extension points without changing normal fresh-scaffold behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Existing .beads/config.yaml or clone-contract issue prefix is inferred before defaulting to prj
- Existing clone-contract bootstrap commands are preserved or represented through scaffold state when adopting an existing repo
- Existing pre-commit local commands are not silently lost; the scaffold exposes or preserves a local hook extension path
- Smoke coverage exercises adoption over an existing Beads repo and the relevant generated candidates
- scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh passes
notes: Follow-up from gloggur-headquarters adoption conflict review. User clarified these are upstream scaffold.sh fixes where possible, not target-project cleanup choices.
close_reason: Updated scripts/scaffold.sh to infer existing Beads prefixes and clone bootstrap commands, preserve existing local pre-commit commands in scaffold candidates, and generate clone contracts from inferred state. Added hook extension support, broadened Beads hook tracked .beads staging, and covered adoption preservation in scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh. Verified with bash scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh.

title: Improve adopted-artifact resolution skill template extraction
description: The scaffold's resolve-adopted-artifacts skill currently allows agents to treat adoption conflicts as mostly local cleanup. In practice, when a scaffold candidate would drop rules from a legacy AGENTS.md, hook script, or Beads contract file, the skill needs to force detailed extraction of reusable template lessons and grill the user on upstream adoption before any archival or local installation. Scope is the upstream template text in bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl and focused smoke coverage that proves the template keeps those stronger instructions.
design: Update bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl so the first-class deliverable is a policy-level adoption ledger and grill-me interview aimed at ../agent-bootstrap template changes. Strengthen AGENTS.md and hook/config conflict handling, require classification of dropped rules as template-worthy/local/obsolete with explicit evidence, and make local file cleanup secondary after upstream decisions. Adjust scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh expectations if needed so future template changes cannot regress the skill's extraction-first behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- Skill template explicitly treats detailed extraction and upstream template grilling as the main focus before cleanup
- AGENTS.md and policy-artifact conflicts require line-item analysis of lost safety rules and reusable scaffold lessons
- The template instructs agents to propose concrete upstream template/script fixes when scaffold behavior would break or change existing functionality
- Smoke coverage asserts the strengthened language is present
- Existing scaffold smoke test passes
notes: Discovered while running /resolve-adopted-artifacts in gloggur-headquarters: AGENTS.scaffold-candidate.md would have dropped no-auto-git rules, hook ownership constraints, stale Beads recovery instructions, and TODO-tracker prohibitions. The user clarified that the skill should primarily analyze these losses for adoption into ../agent-bootstrap templates, not jump to local cleanup.
close_reason: Strengthened resolve-adopted-artifacts upstream skill template so adoption resolution centers detailed loss extraction, upstream scaffold fixes, variables or extension points, and a grill-me adoption interview before any local cleanup. Added smoke assertions and verified with scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh.

title: Make adoption resolution checkpoint questionnaire-first
description: The resolve-adopted-artifacts skill currently forces a large table-heavy extraction checkpoint. In practice, that creates bloated output that is hard to answer, as seen while resolving scaffold adoption conflicts in gloggur-headquarters. The skill should behave more like grill-me: extract evidence internally, then ask concise, grouped questionnaire prompts for only the decisions that need human judgment, with compact summaries instead of wide mandatory tables.
design: Edit bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl. Reframe Step 4 around a questionnaire checkpoint: lead with concise findings, avoid mandatory wide tables, ask focused questions grouped by decision type, and reserve detailed ledgers for internal notes or optional appendices. Preserve the existing safety contract: verify checksums first, do line-item policy inventory internally, stop before file writes, and still route template-worthy findings upstream.
acceptance_criteria:
- The template no longer requires bloated mandatory table output for every conflict
- The checkpoint requires a grill-me/questionnaire style interaction with concise evidence-backed questions
- The workflow still requires line-item policy analysis before asking and explicit approval before writes
- The template keeps the all-at-once conflict resolution and upstream-learning contracts
notes: Triggered by user feedback during /resolve-adopted-artifacts in gloggur-headquarters: the prior checkpoint was too table-heavy and should have been a questionnaire ala grill-me.
close_reason: Updated resolve-adopted-artifacts to use a questionnaire-first extraction interview instead of mandatory table-heavy checkpoints, and added smoke coverage for the new contract.

title: Strengthen resolve-adopted-artifacts value extraction
description: The resolve-adopted-artifacts skill currently tells agents to compare legacy artifacts and scaffold candidates, but a real adoption pass in gloggur-headquarters showed the workflow can still collapse into a replacement checklist. The template needs stronger instructions for extracting reusable workflow value from conflicted agent artifacts, especially AGENTS.md, before proposing any archive/install action.
design: Update bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl. Emphasize artifact-forensics before resolution, require evidence-backed extraction notes for every conflicted policy artifact, require AGENTS.md-specific rule decomposition, and make checkpoint output prioritize extracted value and upstream lessons over file operations. Avoid changing generated local skill copies unless explicitly needed.
acceptance_criteria:
- The skill template explicitly blocks cleanup-first behavior and requires value extraction before archive/install proposals
- AGENTS.md and similar policy artifacts require line-item extraction of commands, restrictions, safety checks, and workflow contracts
- The checkpoint format makes extracted value and proposed upstream/local disposition readable before any file action
- No target-project adoption files are changed
notes: Discovered during gloggur-headquarters /resolve-adopted-artifacts: the initial checkpoint was unreadable and focused too much on installing scaffold candidates, while the user wanted the skill to better mine conflicted AGENTS.md content for reusable upstream improvements.
close_reason: Strengthened resolve-adopted-artifacts template to require value extraction before adoption cleanup.

title: Draft commit message for staged changes
description: Inspect the currently staged git changes and provide a concise conventional commit message that accurately summarizes their scope. No source edits are expected.
design: Use git diff --cached and git status to identify staged file paths and behavioral/doc changes, then produce one high-signal conventional commit message.
acceptance_criteria: - Report a conventional commit message matching the staged changes\n- Do not modify source files\n- Note if no staged changes exist
notes: Requested directly by user in current session.

title: Improve adoption resolution for legacy policy artifacts
description: Current behavior: the resolve-adopted-artifacts skill tells agents to inspect scaffold adoption conflicts, but it can still let policy artifacts such as AGENTS.md be summarized as repo-specific noise. That risks losing safety constraints that are only repo-specific because they predate scaffold standardization. Scope is the upstream universal scaffold templates and smoke test expectations: bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl, bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/AGENTS.md.tmpl, and scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh.
design: Teach the adoption resolver to inventory policy artifacts line by line, split each rule into universal template value, variable/extension-point value, genuinely local preserved constraint, or obsolete noise, and grill the user when that boundary is unclear. Add an AGENTS.md local safety-constraints section as the merge target for constraints that must remain active but do not belong in universal templates. Extend scaffold smoke coverage to assert the new guidance is present in templates and generated AGENTS.md.
acceptance_criteria:
- resolve-adopted-artifacts requires line-item analysis for AGENTS.md and similar policy artifacts
- legacy safety constraints are not classified as obsolete merely because they mention repo-specific values
- generated AGENTS.md has an explicit project-specific safety constraints section for approved local preservation
- scaffold smoke test verifies the new template guidance
notes: Discovered while resolving adoption conflicts in gloggur-headquarters: legacy AGENTS.md contained Beads, git, hook-path, and safety instructions that were partly standardization candidates and partly local operational contracts. Initial checkpoint was too coarse and treated AGENTS.md as one repo-specific artifact instead of decomposing the policy rules.
close_reason: Updated the universal resolve-adopted-artifacts skill to require line-item policy analysis for legacy AGENTS and similar artifacts, added a generated AGENTS.md section for approved local safety constraints, and extended the scaffold smoke test to verify both changes.

title: Keep adoption-conflict files active and fix scaffold next step
description: Scaffold adoption currently preserves pre-existing files by renaming them to *.pre-scaffold.* and then writes generated scaffold files at the active target path. That makes the legacy artifacts inactive before /resolve-adopted-artifacts can inspect them in their real role. The same output also gives conflicting next steps by telling users to run both /resolve-adopted-artifacts and /bootstrap.
design: Update scripts/scaffold.sh so first-run adoption conflicts leave the pre-existing target file in place and write the scaffold candidate to a sidecar path tracked in .agent-scaffold.json. Update resolve-adopted-artifacts guidance and smoke coverage to reflect active legacy targets, scaffold candidates, and conditional next-step output.
acceptance_criteria:
- Adoption conflicts do not rename or deactivate pre-existing target files
- Scaffold-generated candidate content remains available for comparison during /resolve-adopted-artifacts
- Scaffold output does not tell users to run /bootstrap while unresolved adoptionConflicts exist
- Smoke tests cover the new conflict preservation and messaging behavior
notes: Requested directly after observing scaffold output with adoption conflicts in gloggur-headquarters. Existing conflict skill currently assumes preservedBackup plus scaffolded target; that contract must change.

title: Diagnose scaffold adoption next-step messaging
description: Inspect scaffold output and resolve-adopted-artifacts template behavior after a scaffold run reports preserved adoption conflicts and conflicting next steps. Scope is diagnostic unless a small wording or ordering fix is clearly needed.
design: Review scripts/scaffold.sh and bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/skills/resolve-adopted-artifacts.md.tmpl, plus any renderer logic that emits scaffold next-step messages. Avoid unrelated scaffold changes.
acceptance_criteria: - Identify whether scaffold output gives contradictory or unsafe next steps\n- Point to exact file/template responsible\n- If changed, keep the patch scoped and run the fastest configured check
notes: User provided scaffold output from gloggur-headquarters showing preserved adoption conflicts, then both 'run /resolve-adopted-artifacts before re-running scaffold' and 'run /bootstrap'.

title: Rewrite README for clarity and credible onboarding
description: The current README is confusing and overly noisy. Rewrite it so a new contributor can understand what this repository is, how the scaffold/bootstrap flow works, what commands matter, and what the current project status is without reading internal implementation details.
design: Primary edits in README.md. Reference existing repository truth from scripts/, AGENTS.md, .agent-scaffold.json, and .claude durable artifacts where relevant. Keep the README honest about unknown or not-configured project aspects and avoid inventing workflow steps not backed by the repo.
acceptance_criteria: - README explains the repository purpose and current state in plain language\n- README gives accurate setup and command guidance based on the repo\n- README reduces noise, contradictions, and scaffold-internal jargon while preserving necessary contributor context
notes: Evidence: current README.md is described by user as 'bonkers'; repo contains scaffold/bootstrap scripts, AGENTS.md Beads workflow, and .claude plans/context files that should inform a cleaner onboarding doc.

title: Preserve pre-existing project artifacts during scaffold adoption
description: When scripts/scaffold.sh is adopted into an existing project, root or scaffold-adjacent artifacts such as AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and similar files may already exist and contain valuable project-specific instructions. The current scaffold path writes directly to target paths unless the file was previously scaffold-managed, so first-time adoption can silently overwrite pre-existing artifacts. Investigate preservation strategies, define the intended user experience for conflicts, and scope the changes across scripts/scaffold.sh, bootstrap templates, smoke tests, and any state/manifest handling needed to preserve user-authored value.
design: Inspect the current scaffold write path in scripts/scaffold.sh, the manifest model in .agent-scaffold.json, and the generated artifact set from bootstrap-templates/templates/universal/. Evaluate options such as fail-loud conflict detection, sidecar preservation files, merge/import composition, or first-run adoption modes. Identify exact touchpoints in scripts/scaffold.sh, scripts/smoke-test-scaffold.sh, template files for AGENTS/CLAUDE routing, and any new manifest schema needed to distinguish pre-existing adopted artifacts from scaffold-owned files. Non-goal: implementing broad semantic merges for arbitrary markdown without a clear deterministic contract.
acceptance_criteria: - Existing valuable artifacts are not silently destroyed on first scaffold adoption\n- The scaffold behavior for pre-existing conflicts is deterministic and explainable\n- Focused regression coverage captures first-run adoption scenarios for AGENTS/CLAUDE-like files
notes: Evidence: current scripts/scaffold.sh only protects files already recorded in .agent-scaffold.json by checksum, and otherwise overwrites destinations like AGENTS.md on first scaffold run. Discovery requested by user before implementation; this issue tracks the design and potential landing work.

title: Investigate false-positive Beads readiness after scaffold bootstrap
description: Running scripts/scaffold.sh inside a freshly initialized git repo can report 'Beads bootstrap verified' or attempt bootstrap, yet the resulting repo still fails bd status --json because local Beads state is not fully usable. This is separate from the partial-commit leak fix and needs a focused repair to make new scaffolded repos genuinely Beads-ready or to defer initialization more explicitly.
design: Reproduce scaffold output in a fresh git repo, inspect the interaction between scaffold.sh, bd bootstrap, .beads/config.yaml, and the embedded Dolt metadata, then choose whether scaffold should initialize differently or present a clearer deferred workflow.
acceptance_criteria: - Fresh scaffolded git repos do not claim Beads readiness unless bd status --json succeeds\n- The chosen initialization/defer path is documented by the script output\n- Regression coverage proves the chosen behavior
notes: Discovered while fixing agb-1z7: smoke-test work showed fresh scaffolded repos can still fail bd status even after bootstrap attempts.

title: Fix Beads issues snapshot leaking past git commit
description: Beads task-state changes are being exported into .beads/issues.jsonl but can remain outside the commit they belong to. Fix the git hook or scaffolded hook flow so the exported snapshot is included in the same git commit that triggers it instead of being left behind as a follow-up change.
design: Inspect the active git hook configuration, the scaffolded hook scripts, and commit timing around bd export. Reproduce the leak in a minimal repo or fixture, then update hook/scaffold logic and regression coverage so commits reliably include the refreshed snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: - A git commit that triggers a Beads export includes the resulting .beads/issues.jsonl change in that same commit\n- No leftover tracked Beads snapshot remains after the commit completes\n- A regression check covers the fixed behavior
notes: Observed in this repository after recent commits; existing pre-commit export logic appears intended to auto-stage the snapshot but the leak still occurs.

title: Review staged Beads snapshot after scaffold refactor commit
description: Inspect the currently staged generated change in .beads/issues.jsonl after commit ae25d1b to determine whether the change should have been auto-staged and committed with that work, and identify the mechanism gap if it was missed.
design: Compare HEAD, index, and working tree around .beads/issues.jsonl plus relevant git hook and Beads export/bootstrap integration paths. No product code changes unless a concrete fix is approved.
acceptance_criteria: - Explain what the staged change contains and why it appeared after the last commit\n- Determine whether the previous commit should have included it automatically\n- Identify the exact mechanism or workflow gap responsible
notes: Evidence will come from git status, cached diff, last commit stat, and repository hook/config inspection.

title: Backfill retroactive Beads tasks for existing git history
description: Retroactively create high-quality Beads tasks for each historical git commit in this repository, with exact commit timestamp backdating and a retcon label, without changing git history.
design: Inspect git log in chronological order, analyze each commit diff, create one Beads issue per commit, backdate created/updated/closed timestamps to the commit timestamp, and close each issue immediately. Check existing Beads data first to avoid duplicate retcon tasks.
acceptance_criteria: - Every historical git commit without an existing retcon issue gets one corresponding Beads task\n- Each created task is labeled retcon and backdated to the exact commit timestamp for created_at, updated_at, and closed_at\n- A final report lists the number of tasks created and any commits skipped as already covered
notes: Requested via /fabricate-beads-history on 2026-04-28. Need to verify whether earlier runs already created any retroactive tasks before backfilling.

title: Draft conventional commit message for current staged changes
description: Inspect the currently staged git changes and provide a conventional commit message that matches the actual scope and intent.
design: Use git diff --cached to inspect staged files and summarize the change as a conventional commit without modifying code or git state.
acceptance_criteria: - Identify whether staged changes exist\n- Produce a conventional commit header\n- Include a concise body when the staged scope warrants it
notes: Initial probe: git diff --cached returned no output; verify staged state before drafting the message.

title: Fold leaked beads export-state into prior commit and stop restaging it
description: A Beads-generated .beads/export-state.json change was staged at commit submission time instead of being included in the commit that triggered it. Fix the current leaked change and adjust the workflow/config so this file does not keep becoming a post-commit annoyance.
design: Inspect the last commit, beads export-state generation path, git ignore/tracking rules, and commit hooks. Amend or otherwise repair the prior commit to include the leaked file if appropriate, then change the repo config or hook behavior so export-state does not reappear as a staged leftover after commits.
acceptance_criteria:
- The leaked .beads/export-state.json change is no longer left staged separately from the commit it belongs to
- Future commits do not leave .beads/export-state.json staged as a follow-up annoyance
- Any hook/config change preserves the intended beads workflow
notes: Observed immediately after commit bdb58a2 with only A .beads/export-state.json left staged.

title: Summarize staged scaffold/bootstrap changes for commit
description: Review the current staged scaffold/bootstrap change set and produce a conventional commit message that reflects the actual scope. No code changes; scope is limited to inspecting staged diffs and naming the change accurately.
design: Inspect git status and cached diff for scaffold, bootstrap, skill, template, and beads metadata updates. Derive a single commit message that matches the staged contract without expanding scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- Commit subject uses conventional commit format
- Subject reflects the staged scaffold/bootstrap work
- Body captures the main why/scope in a few lines if needed
notes: Staged diff shows scaffold/bootstrap split, new bootstrap/scaffold scripts, skill/template updates, and beads metadata changes.

title: Replace interactive bootstrap prompts with inferred bootstrap skill
description: Change the shell bootstrap entrypoint so it no longer interrogates the user for project metadata and commands. It should infer what it can and generate the bootstrap artifacts non-interactively. Add a new bootstrap skill that performs the deeper project interrogation by reading the codebase and using the grill-me approach to fully define any remaining project context.
design: Update scripts/bootstrap.sh and universal templates. Introduce a bootstrap skill template and generated copies where appropriate. Adjust docs and workflow references so the shell command handles generation while the new bootstrap skill handles agent-side discovery and project-context refinement.
acceptance_criteria:
- bootstrap.sh runs without the old question-and-answer prompts for project metadata
- a new bootstrap skill is generated and documented
- the bootstrap skill explains how to inspect an existing codebase and derive project context using grill-me style interrogation of the code rather than the human
notes: Created from user request to separate file generation from project interrogation and move the latter into a new skill.

title: Explain repository structure and architecture
description: Inspect the repository, identify its current structure, purpose, and architecture, and provide a concise explanation grounded in the existing files and conventions.
design: Review README.md, AGENTS.md, top-level configuration, and src/ if present. Summarize module responsibilities, workflows, and notable gaps without changing code.
acceptance_criteria:
- Claimed issue exists for this exploration work
- Project summary covers purpose, structure, and current state
- Notes any missing or not-yet-configured areas discovered during inspection
notes: Created because bd ready --json returned no available tasks for this explanatory walkthrough.

title: Unify scaffold apply and refresh behavior around scaffold state
description: Refactor the scaffold flow so both initial application and later refreshes are driven by the same scaffold-state model instead of diverging command paths. Remove the obsolete sync-bootstrap workflow, align harness skills and templates around the new refresh behavior, and simplify documentation and tests to match the state-driven contract.
design: Update scaffold state artifacts, harness skill docs, AGENTS/CLAUDE templates, scaffold.sh, and smoke tests so apply and refresh share one implementation path with consistent semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- Scaffold apply and refresh run through the same state-driven logic
- Obsolete sync-bootstrap artifacts are removed from generated outputs and templates
- Documentation and smoke tests reflect the unified scaffold contract
